name: Deepgram API
what_it_does: Deepgram's speech-to-text API offering fast, accurate audio transcription with real-time streaming, speaker diarization, sentiment analysis, and language detection.
use_cases: Transcribing audio files for agent processing and analysis
Real-time speech-to-text for voice-enabled AI agents
Call center audio analysis and automated quality scoring
Building voice interfaces for conversational AI systems
not_for: Very low-budget projects (costs add up for long audio)
Languages with limited training data (accuracy varies by language)
best_when: An agent needs to transcribe speech with high accuracy and speed, especially for real-time voice applications or batch audio processing.
avoid_when: You need text-to-speech, offline transcription, or have very tight latency requirements beyond Deepgram's capabilities.

auth.notes: API key with configurable permissions (Member, Admin, Owner). Keys can be scoped to specific capabilities. Simple bearer token for agents. Project-scoped keys available.

pricing.notes: Very competitive pricing for transcription. $200 free credit covers extensive testing. Credit card required after credits. Batch processing is cheapest.

performance.rate_limits: 100 concurrent streams for real-time, generous batch limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with err_code and err_msg. Real-time WebSocket errors are clearly typed. Error documentation covers common issues thoroughly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transcription is a processing operation — not idempotent by nature. Callback URLs can be used to avoid duplicate processing.
known_agent_gotchas: Real-time streaming uses WebSocket protocol — different from REST, agents need WS client
Audio format must be specified correctly — wrong mimetype causes silent failures
Diarization (speaker labels) adds latency — don't enable unless needed
Callback URLs for async transcription must be publicly accessible
Smart formatting changes transcription text — agents must account for punctuation injection
Language detection has minimum audio length requirements
